title: Jessie Buckley Wins Best Actress Oscar for Hamnet
text: Jessie Buckley secured the Academy Award for Best Actress at the 98th Oscars ceremony on March 15, 2026, at the Dolby Theatre for her portrayal of Agnes Shakespeare in director Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet. This marks Buckley's first Oscar win. She was previously nominated for Best Supporting Actress in 2022 for The Lost Daughter. Buckley had dominated the competitive season leading up to the Oscars, accumulating major acting awards from the Actor Awards, BAFTA Film Awards, Critics Choice Awards, and Golden Globes. Her performance reportedly stood out against other strong contenders including Rose Byrne, Kate Hudson, Renate Reinsve, and Emma Stone. Buckley plays the wife of William Shakespeare and mother of the film's titular character Hamnet. Director Zhao emphasized Buckley's authenticity for the role, noting there was no vanity in her and that vanity is the number-one enemy of authenticity.
